name: scientific-visualization
tagline: Meta-skill that orchestrates matplotlib/seaborn/plotly to produce journal-ready figures with panel layouts, error bars, colorblind-safe.
verdict: Safe, well-scoped, and highly actionable: no network/exec/secret risk, clear trigger description, concrete code examples throughout. Weak on operational discipline — never declares or checks its matplotlib/seaborn/pandas/plotly dependencies, gives no guidance for missing scripts or failed imports, and duplicates large seaborn code blocks inline despite.

highlights.summary: No failure handling anywhere: e.g. line 35 imports style_presets/figure_export with no check the scripts exist or import cleanly.
Full boxplot+stripplot code block duplicated near-verbatim (line 95-101 and line 396-402) instead of deferring to references/matplotlib_examples.md, which is separately listed.
SKILL.md is 776 lines / ~25KB, over the ~500-line/25KB budget but under 2x, despite having reference files for overflow content.
